rationale: Ran the full Bead-2 Verification checklist fresh against 62cd6df3. All seven steps pass: (V1) TestValidateSpec_ImpactedDomainSeverityMatchesPlanApprove PASS-line grep exit 0; (V2) TestValidateSpec_ADRTouchpointExtractionBoundary PASS-line grep exit 0; (V3) go test -count=1 ./internal/validate ok (126s, uncached); (V4) go test -count=1 ./internal/approve ok (74s, uncached); (V5) branch-built self-check go build -o /tmp/ms110b2 ./cmd/mindspec && /tmp/ms110b2 validate spec 110-... -> 'all checks passed', exit 0; (V6) doc-sync grep for architecture.md exit 0; (V7) go build ./... exit 0. Confirmed the parity claims are literally true in-source: R5 makes the identical normalizeImpactedDomains(nil, root, "", impacted) call + impacted-domains-resolve code as plan.go:142-144 and reproduces plan.go:126's load-error handling verbatim; R6 uses the identical newMemoStore(adrStoreForSpecFn(root, specDir)) store as plan.go:156 / divergence.go:137; the R6 section body is the same parseSections(content) map (spec.go:50/71). git status clean, HEAD unchanged, /tmp binary removed.
findings: V1/V2 named-test PASS-line greps both exit 0 (anchored on '--- PASS: <TestName>').
V3 go test -count=1 ./internal/validate: ok 126.153s (fresh, cache bypassed) — no shared-helper regression in the existing plan-approve impacted-domains-resolve/adr-cite-missing tests.
V4 go test -count=1 ./internal/approve: ok 74.236s (fresh) — spec-approve inherits the new ValidateSpec failures with zero internal/approve source change.

V5 branch-built self-check: freshly built ./cmd/mindspec binary validates 110's own spec ('all checks passed', exit 0) — anchored touchpoints to existing ADRs resolve, bare-prose ADR-0040/ADR-0030 not matched, single workflow Impacted-Domain resolves; self-consistency holds.
V6/V7: doc-sync region present in .mindspec/domains/workflow/architecture.md (grep -qxF exit 0); go build ./... exit 0.

Parity grounded: R5 call site is byte-identical to plan.go (helper loadImpactedDomains, call normalizeImpactedDomains(nil, root, "", impacted), code impacted-domains-resolve, and the errors.Unwrap/os.IsNotExist load-error guard); R6 store construction newMemoStore(adrStoreForSpecFn(root, specDir)) matches plan.go:156 and divergence.go:137 exactly.
R7c boundary holds: the two checks emit only impacted-domains-resolve / adr-touchpoint-missing; no adr-coverage-* or adr-cite-irrelevant path is reachable at spec-approve (existence/resolution only).
